svg.aria-label: Direct CV conditioning perfboard placement map on a square 2.54 pitch grid
svg.text: Direct CV conditioning perfboard placement map

One conditioning channel on a square 2.54-style perfboard grid. This matches the same layout model as the CV output perfboard map: top rail, bottom rail, on-board part labels, and only node callouts outside the board.
Main placement on square pad grid
Top rail is 3V3 on row A. Bottom rail is GND on row J. The off-board jack feeds SW1, and SW1 selects exactly one of the three conditioning paths before the shared ADC protection/filter stage.
The three switch positions feed the same protected ADC node through different conditioning paths. SW1 must connect the jack tip to only one path at a time.
Route J1 directly to the merge node, then through R5, D1/D2, and C1 into AD1/AD2/AD3.
Use R1 and R2 as a 2:1 divider, then feed the same protected node and ADC filter stage.
Use the MCP6002-I/P stage with the 100k / 100k mid-rail bias so the ADC only sees the shifted 0-3.3 V range.
Inset: SW1 wiring and switch type
Use a single-pole three-throw selector: a panel-mount 1P3T rotary switch is the safest choice, or an SP3T ON-ON-ON toggle if its datasheet confirms one common plus three selectable throws.
Common or wiper terminal: connect to J1 tip only.
Throw for direct mode: connect to B1, which lands straight on the shared merge node.
Throw for attenuated mode: connect to D1, feeding the top of R1 so the D-row node is halved by R1 and R2.
Throw for bipolar mode: connect to G1, feeding the MCP6002-I/P input stage and its 1.65 V bias network.
Preferred behavior: break-before-make so adjacent positions do not momentarily short two paths together during switching.
Coordinates below follow the main board above. The jack and switch are off-board panel parts; the perfboard carries the selectable conditioning network and the final ADC protection/filter stage.
Use a 1P3T selector. Jack tip goes to the switch common/wiper, the three throws go to B1 direct, D1 attenuated, and G1 bipolar, and panel ground returns to J1.
The D-row attenuator node then routes upward to the shared merge point on B9.
This forms the 2:1 divider used when SW1 selects the attenuated 0-10 V path.
Shown using one op-amp section. Pin 8 goes to 3V3, pin 4 goes to GND, and the output rises to the shared merge node.
These two 100k parts create the 1.65 V bias node used by the optional bipolar conditioning path.
All three selectable paths merge before R5, then the final ADC protection/filter stage is common.
D13 then links down to J13 so the capacitor stays near the ADC node while the real ground bus stays on row J.
These are the two 1N4148 clamp diodes that keep the ADC node inside the safe range near the Waveshare ADC input.
Take the conditioned node from B15 to one of the direct ADC inputs. Keep the ground return on the same board ground rail.
